title: Lemony Crunchy Vietnamese Pork Banhsu
description: A citrusy, crunchy, and cozy Vietnamese dish featuring the flavors of rose water, served in a traditional cataplana vessel. This diabetic-friendly version of banh mi replaces traditional carbs with healthier alternatives.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine pork shoulder, brown rice flour, almond meal, rose water, lemon juice, grated ginger, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to combine.
Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the pork mixture and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Transfer the pork mixture to a cataplana vessel and cover with a lid.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C).
While the pork is cooking, prepare the crunchy topping by combining crushed almonds, fresh cilantro, sliced scallions, pickled carrots, and pickled daikon in a bowl.
Once the pork is done, top it with the crunchy mixture and serve immediately.
Drizzle with sriracha sauce, if desired.
Serve hot in a traditional Vietnamese banh mi vessel, garnished with additional cilantro and scallions if desired.
